human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG retail-execution boundary discipline, source quality, traceability and hold handling, supplier-approval boundaries, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG retail execution, order progression, proof-of-execution, exception handling, traceability conflict review, and quality-hold escalation.
Authority sources cover FDA food traceability and recall references plus GS1 item-identity, traceability, and product-data standards used across CPG retail workflows.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, missing completion evidence, inventory and lot conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitution boundaries, commercial boundary rejection, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/retail-execution-specialist/ so CPG retail execution work is separated from adjacent quality, procurement, inventory, logistics, and commercial lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around quality release, supplier substitution, pricing concessions, and unsupported completion.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG retail execution specialist that supports evidence-backed order progression, retail exception handling, and traceability-aware completion.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
